title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
summary: The Trump administration, which took a noninterventionist approach to artificial intelligence, is now discussing imposing oversight on A.I. models before they are made publicly available.

title: Attacks Threaten to Reignite Iran Conflict
summary: Also, the stars arrive at the Met Gala. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
summary: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.

title: Met Gala 2026 Red Carpet Photos: All the Looks
summary: See the arrivals on the cobblestone carpet.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Quietly Attends His First Met Gala
summary: The former nerd completes his fashion Met-amorphosis.

title: Mayor Mamdani Highlights Fashion Workers as He Skips Met Gala
summary: New York’s mayor highlighted the seamstresses, tailors and other workers in the fashion industry.

title: Soil at D.C. Golf Course Where East Wing Debris Was Dumped Contains Toxic Metals
summary: The National Park Service said the dump of debris, between the fourth and ninth holes, does not exceed environmental limits. Opponents disagree.

title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
summary: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.

title: Supreme Court Agrees to Fast-Track Louisiana Voting Map Decision
summary: Louisiana voters who successfully challenged the state’s voting map as an illegal racial gerrymander had asked the justices to quickly return the case to the lower courts, clearing the way for a new map.

title: New York Times Wins 3 Pulitzer Prizes
summary: The Washington Post won the award for public service, considered the most prestigious of the Pulitzers, for its coverage of the Trump administration’s overhaul of federal agencies.

title: Pulitzer Prizes: 2026 Winners List
summary: Here is the full list of winners and finalists.

title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
summary: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.

title: Home on the Range No More: Trump Wants Bison Gone
summary: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.

title: More Than 150 Wind Projects Stall as Pentagon Delays Reviews
summary: The delays, which companies say have worsened significantly in recent weeks, are the latest step in the Trump administration’s efforts to block wind power.

title: Trump Administration Orders Rapid End to Hunting Regulations on Federal Lands
summary: Internal Interior Department documents show parks are imposing sweeping changes like expanding areas where firearms can be used and allowing game to be cleaned in restrooms.

title: Chasing Stardom in Korea Nearly Destroyed Ejae. Then Came ‘KPop Demon Hunters.’
summary: Ejae, the movie’s breakout star, was the face of K-pop in 2025. Facing questions of art, national belonging and authenticity, she wants to be much more.

title: The Return for These Investors Isn’t Money, It’s More Affordable Housing
summary: Local governments are trying to create housing that is permanently affordable by investing directly in construction. They are rewriting how housing programs have traditionally operated.

title: A 22,000-Home Community Is Being Built in a 5,000-Person Town
summary: Chatham Park, a master-planned community in Pittsboro, N.C., is expected to expand the town to 60,000 people when completed.

title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Settle ‘It Ends With Us’ Lawsuit
summary: Ms. Lively had accused Mr. Baldoni of waging a smear campaign against her after she complained of harassment when making a film together. He had denied her accusation.

title: How a Hantavirus Infection Feels: ‘You Just Can’t Get the Air In’
summary: Doctors say that the virus, which is rare, can quickly progress from flulike symptoms to something far more serious.

title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
summary: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed one case and are investigating five others.

title: Elon Musk’s Lawyers Ask OpenAI’s President Why He Is Worth $30 Billion
summary: The legal team implied in a federal trial that Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president and co-founder, was driven by greed rather than building safe A.I.

title: A Movie About Women Who Love Their Work? Groundbreaking.
summary: It turns out that some women do, in fact, dream of labor, at least the kind that comes with a sense of mastery, agency and glamour.

title: Speak, Yuppie
summary: The young urban professionals of the 1980s changed our cities, our politics and our meritocratic work lives.

title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
summary: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.

title: Four Epstein Victims Ask N.Y. Lawmakers to Open His Estate to Lawsuits
summary: New York State law prevents people from seeking punitive damages from the estate of someone who has died.

title: Colorado Snowstorm Could Be Its Biggest All Year
summary: Snow in May is not rare in the Rocky Mountains. But this year, any snow at all is unusual.

title: California Seeks ‘Historic’ Fine Against State Farm: Just $2 Million
summary: Regulators say the penalty is the highest ever sought, but a cap on each violation would soften the blow for California’s largest insurer.
